microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user prefers food served warm without extra delay. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE fatigue increases desire for comfort and hotter food is more satisfying. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 1 min BECAUSE efficiency outweighs maximizing warmth. IF any dining context AND neutral AND fruit or cold dessert THEN no microwave BECAUSE these foods are intended to be served cold. IF social setting on the left or right AND relaxed with ample time AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user keeps a normal warming routine even when socializing. IF watching television or working on a laptop/tablet in front AND physically fatigued AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE comfort is prioritized when attention is divided and energy is low.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE there is no important visual target to preserve. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user wants to keep the social partner visible. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the right AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE preserving that line of sight matters. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user wants an unobstructed view of the screen. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND in a hurry AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE task visibility still outweighs speed. IF social setting on either side AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion or minimize right occlusion matching partner location BECAUSE this user’s tie-break favors maintaining the external visual target even when tired.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE the user is comfort-oriented and has slow mouth opening. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND easy foods like fruit or dessert THEN medium BECAUSE the user accepts some speed increase when efficiency pressure is high. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE fatigue increases desire for predictability and comfort. IF social setting on the left or right AND neutral AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE conservative motion feels safer and less disruptive around others. IF watching television or working on a laptop/tablet in front AND in a hurry AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE divided attention and time pressure support modest acceleration but not fast motion. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND messy or sauced foods THEN slow BECAUSE careful movement reduces spill risk. IF any dining context AND physically energetic AND dry easy-to-acquire foods THEN medium BECAUSE the user tolerates somewhat quicker service when feeling capable.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND larger or thicker pieces of meat THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE narrower bites are easier given limited mouth opening. IF any dining context AND neutral AND soft items that often fail to stay on the fork THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE acquisition reliability can outweigh bite narrowness for fragile foods. IF social setting on the left or right AND neutral AND foods prone to failed acquisition THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE avoiding repeated failed attempts is especially valuable around others. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND larger or tougher foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE fatigue makes easier oral transfer more important. IF watching television or working on a laptop/tablet in front AND in a hurry AND easy stable foods THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user accepts a larger bite to improve speed and success. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND foods that can form oversized bites THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE comfort remains the stable preference when time pressure is low.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user can access the web interface and prefers reliable bite transfer. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND easy stable foods THEN no BECAUSE the user temporarily accepts more automation to reduce interaction time. IF social setting on the left or right AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE avoiding empty-fork transfers matters in front of others. IF watching television or working on a laptop/tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN no BECAUSE divided attention makes extra confirmation burdensome. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN no BECAUSE this user’s tie-break under fatigue is to minimize effort. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND foods prone to failed acquisition like slippery or irregular items THEN yes BECAUSE reliability overrides effort when failure risk is high. IF relaxed with ample time in any context AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user prefers dependable operation when not rushed.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot reliably lean forward. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE fatigue further reduces ability to reach toward the utensil. IF social setting on the left or right AND neutral AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE physical constraints outweigh possible social discomfort. IF watching television or working on a laptop/tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot depend on trunk movement while attention is divided. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE it is the most feasible and efficient mode for this body profile. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the preference is stable due to physical necessity.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE the default transfer mode is inside mouth transfer. IF social setting on the left or right AND neutral AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user still requires inside mouth transfer despite social context. IF watching television or working on a laptop/tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE divided attention does not change the physical need for inside mouth transfer. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE fatigue strengthens the need for direct transfer. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user cannot safely rely on reaching forward. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE extra time does not change the user’s movement limitations.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN speech + LED BECAUSE multimodal cues are easiest to notice without requiring effort. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any food THEN LED BECAUSE a visual cue is less disruptive to media listening. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN speech BECAUSE the user may not be looking at the robot and audio is more reliable. IF social setting on the left or right AND neutral AND any food THEN LED BECAUSE quieter signaling is less socially intrusive. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN speech + LED BECAUSE redundant cues reduce missed prompts when attention and energy are low. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any food THEN speech BECAUSE the fastest unmistakable prompt supports efficient flow. IF relaxed with ample time in any context AND any food THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user prefers maximum clarity when speed pressure is low.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and prefers low-effort control. IF social setting on the left or right AND neutral AND any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE open-mouth detection may be confused by conversation and button use is infeasible. IF social setting on the left or right AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE this user’s tie-break under fatigue is to minimize effort and avoid unreliable cues. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE attention is divided and spontaneous mouth movements are less intentional. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE task engagement makes explicit readiness signaling less reliable. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND any food THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user prefers some control when the environment is quiet. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE efficiency temporarily outweighs explicit user-triggered initiation.

IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any drink THEN open mouth BECAUSE it provides low-effort readiness control without button use. IF social setting on the left or right AND neutral AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE talking can interfere with open-mouth interpretation and buttons are not feasible. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE divided attention makes passive progression easier. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND in a hurry AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE efficient hands-free interaction is preferred. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE minimizing effort is the consistent tie-break. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND any drink THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user likes retaining some timing control when conditions are calm. IF any dining context AND neutral AND thick or awkward drinks requiring more preparation THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user may want explicit readiness before sip transfer.

IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE open mouth is not a natural readiness cue for wiping and buttons are infeasible. IF social setting on the left or right AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE it avoids awkward signaling during conversation. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user prefers minimal interruption. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE attention is already occupied. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE low-effort automation is strongly preferred. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the preference is stable for this task. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE it is the fastest feasible initiation method.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE redundant cues clearly indicate when to take the bite or sip. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE extra clarity helps when reactions are slower. detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND feeding THEN perception BECAUSE it is reliable and avoids impossible button presses. detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE head-gesture perception is unreliable for this user and buttons are infeasible. IF social setting on the left or right AND neutral AND drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE explicit completion signaling would be awkward and difficult. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the preference is stable given the user’s motor limits. IF any dining context AND thicker drinks or slower sipping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the long wait setting can accommodate slower completion without requiring gestures.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND mouth wiping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE head-gesture perception is unreliable and buttons are infeasible. IF social setting on the left or right AND neutral AND mouth wiping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE it avoids awkward explicit signals. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND mouth wiping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user prefers the least effortful completion method. retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN no BECAUSE the user prefers efficiency when no important view is being blocked. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE preserving the front view matters. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user wants the screen to remain visible between transfers. IF social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE reducing prolonged obstruction supports social comfort. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN no BECAUSE this user’s tie-break under fatigue is to reduce total meal duration. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any food THEN no BECAUSE efficiency outweighs occlusion concerns. IF relaxed with ample time in a social or screen-focused context AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user is willing to trade time for better visibility.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND dippable foods with sauce THEN more BECAUSE the user enjoys richer flavor when mess risk is low. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND dippable foods with sauce THEN less BECAUSE lighter dipping reduces time and mess. IF social setting on the left or right AND neutral AND dippable foods with sauce THEN less BECAUSE the user wants to avoid messy transfers around others. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND dippable foods with sauce THEN less BECAUSE cleaner, simpler bites are easier to manage. IF watching television or working on a laptop/tablet in front AND neutral AND dippable foods with sauce THEN less BECAUSE the user wants to minimize distraction from drips or cleanup. IF any dining context AND neutral AND desserts or fruits without intended dipping THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user does not add sauce when it is not part of the food.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user may need extra time for slow mouth opening and paced interaction. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE fatigue increases response time and the user wants a large safety margin. IF social setting on the left or right AND neutral AND any food THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE conversation can delay readiness and completion. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any food THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user is somewhat distracted but still wants reasonable flow. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE task engagement can substantially delay intervention. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any food THEN 10 sec BECAUSE efficiency temporarily outweighs waiting for manual intervention. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically energetic AND easy foods THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user can keep pace with a more automated rhythm.
